content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

February 25, 1992

Honorable S. Joseph Simitian  
Councilmember  
City of Palo Alto  
1800 Guinda Street  
Palo Alto, CA 94303

Re: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. A-92-090

Dear Councilmember Simitian:

This is in response to your request for confirmation of telephone advice provided to you on February 4, 1992, regarding the propriety of using campaign funds to make a contribution to a federal candidate under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

This letter confirms that your letter dated February 5, 1992 accurately summarizes the advice I provided to you on February 4, 1992. As I stated in our telephone conversation, generally, any expenditure of campaign funds must be, at a minimum, reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.2 (Section 89512.) Consequently, so long as the contribution to a federal candidate is reasonably related to a political purpose, the expenditure is permitted. (See, Dorman Advice Letter, No. I-91-253.)

Please note that the provisions of the Act which were formerly interpreted to restrict the transfer of campaign funds were found invalid in Service Employees International Union, et al. v. Fair Political Practices Commission (E.D. Calif., 1990) 747

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Expenditures which confer a substantial personal benefit must be directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. (Section 89512.) Additionally, some expenditures must meet the "directly related" standard by statute.

F.Supp. 580 (affirmed by the U.S. Court of Appeals, 9th Circuit, Case Nos. 89-15771, 90-16200 and 90-16372). Should the provisions of Proposition 73 invalidated in the case be found constitutionally valid in the future, the advice contained in this letter may no longer be accurate.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.3

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JWW:aa

3 Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a cost of 10¢ per page.
